overall_verdict: Worth it. A small, repo-local guardrail (one stdlib hook + settings wiring + CLAUDE.md) now makes the repo's own discipline self-enforcing for the agent at the commit chokepoint, without touching the engine or the shipped plugin. The ceremony of routing it through a workstream paid for itself: it forced the design forks (scope, commit-match, repo-only boundary) to be decided explicitly and surfaced a real regex bug during the dry-run before it could land.

done_when.clause: (a) PreToolUse hook denies git commit without a working-phase iteration, allows it with one, clear reason
done_when.evidence: .claude/hooks/require-workstream-commit.py; dry-run showed deny (no working iteration / empty repo) and allow (working iteration present), with an actionable deny reason.

done_when.clause: (b) Hook in repo-scoped .claude/, not plugin/, no engine change
done_when.evidence: Files added only under .claude/ and repo root; git diff touches nothing under plugin/ and no lume engine module.

done_when.clause: (c) Repo-root CLAUDE.md documents the way of working
done_when.evidence: CLAUDE.md states the rule, the satisfy-path, the enforcement, and the operator-only accept/merge gates.

done_when.clause: (d) Carve-outs correct (only absence-of-working blocks; operator manual edits and out-of-repo files unaffected)
done_when.evidence: Hook only acts on Bash git-commit; non-commit Bash and non-Bash tools pass; the hook fires on agent tool calls only; predicate keys on .lume state under the repo root.

done_when.clause: (e) Verified deny and allow via dry-run
done_when.evidence: 8 payloads dry-run: allow, pass-through (status/Edit/commit-graph/commit-tree), deny (accepted iteration / empty repo); word-boundary bug found and fixed.

done_when.clause: (f) This workstream's own changes committed through it
done_when.evidence: E1 and E2 committed during their iterations' working phases on branch workstream/enforce-workstream-gate.

stage_verdicts.cost: Two iterations of recording mechanism, scope, and forks.
stage_verdicts.saved: Locked the commit-only scope and repo-only boundary before any code, avoiding a heavier edit-gating design and a plugin-coupled mistake.

stage_verdicts.cost: Hook script + settings + CLAUDE.md, plus a dry-run harness.
stage_verdicts.saved: Dry-run caught the commit-graph/commit-tree false-positive pre-merge; CLAUDE.md pairs prose guidance with the hard stop.

carry_forwards: The gate activates only at session start (hooks load then), so it takes effect for the operator's NEXT session after this merges.
It is a chokepoint, not a sandbox: raw Bash redirects can mutate files; only the commit is gated. Revisit if evasion is ever observed.
The commit-match is heuristic regex; an obfuscated commit (e.g. via a shell variable) could slip it - acceptable for honest guidance.
lume close for this workstream is left to the operator, to pair with their accept/merge authority after the PR lands.
